title: Neohintonia monantha
intro: Neohintonia monantha, also known as Monantho, is a plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions, including wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Neohintonia monantha include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neohintonia monantha include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and gastrointestinal problems.
taxonomy: Neohintonia monantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Neohintonia, which is a monotypic genus. The plant is native to Mexico. Neohintonia monantha is classified as an evergreen shrub. It is a rare and little-known species within its genus.
uses: Neohintonia monantha has been reported to have various medicinal properties, primarily in traditional medicine.
